structural_transitions.after_commit: The next authoritative view will expose the real post-wp surgery workbench, so you do not need to mentally simulate now what will happen.
structural_transitions.decision: Probe this tactic if you want to test entry into the post-wp surgery phase.
structural_transitions.if_wrong_after_commit: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.
structural_transitions.why_here: The current pRHL seq-cut exposes a large postcondition. Probe `wp.` if you want to test entry into the real post-wp suffix/surgery workbench.

checks: Read coverage and missing live facts before committing a seq cut.
A runnable seq candidate is not evidence that the invariant is strong enough.

translation_facts: Decorations identify the program memory from which a term is read.
Pretty-printer memory names and introduced proof-context names can differ.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: Sampling order note: a paired same-distribution sample is still live (z{1} <$ dblock at statement 10 with z{2} <$ dblock at statement 10), while one-sided sample(s) are also visible (t0{1} <$ dpoly_out at statement 16; r{1} <$ dpoly_in at statement 23). Eas...

avoid: This is a branch-local/suffix alignment problem; a fresh restart throws away useful opener and invariant work.
One side has sampling or instrumentation before the suffix; use `rcond`, `swap`, or one-sided `rnd` before plain `wp`.
`sim` is usually too late/too strong before the guard and suffix shape are aligned.
deep_focus.confidence: medium — The current pRHL goal exposes a bad-event/instrumented branch together with sampling or one-sided suffix evidence; this is the shape where experts use `case`, `rcondt/rcondf`, `swap`, indexed `wp`, `conseq`, or one-sided `rnd` before local SMT. The fast-track tactic is schematic, so confidence is capped until the current view or an inspect result supplies concrete terms.
deep_focus.fast_track_probe: try `case: (<current branch guard>).` first — Use only when the visible goal has a concrete guard controlling the local branch.
repair_if_fails: syntax → inspect tactic_forms for swap, rcondt, rcondf, wp, conseq, rnd, or eager
frontier → inspect align or goal_info for statement indexes before retrying
invariant → use undo_to_checkpoint {} and choose the checkpoint before the call/loop invariant that should carry the missing guard or size fact
lemma → inspect lemma_hints or lookup_symbol for the local size/nth/bad-event lemma
route → downgrade this surgery route only after a smaller prefix and relevant tactic_forms fail
deep_focus.route: experts decompose this shape as case split then rcond swap wp conseq → local smt.
toolbox: `case: (<which guard>)` — split the divergent branch (YOU pick the condition).
`rcondt{i} N` / `rcondf{i} N` — force a branch (YOU pick t/f); for a loop guard, `while(true); auto`.
`swap [a..b] c` — line up statement order across the two sides.
`wp` / `wp -N -N` — absorb suffix statements (from the end) before `call`/`sim`.
`conseq(:_==> ={<the few equal vars>})` then `sim` — weaken to the equal prefix and auto-close it.
where: setup before the frontier (positions 1–8) — absorb with `sp`/`wp`: 8 setup statement(s): p0 <- p; nap <- p0; (n, a, p1) <- nap; ... (5 more)
deep_focus.why_now: current pRHL state looks past the main opener: the useful next work is local branch/suffix surgery, not changing proof family.
deep_focus.yours: which condition to `case` on, which way each guard resolves, the sample coupling (e.g. `t0{1} = t1{2}`), the smt lemmas.

reference.note: Panels collapsed for focus. The essentials for THIS phase are in the focus above; the rest were noise here (a flat view of all of them is what disorients). If you genuinely need a precise field from one, ask with `inspect_context`.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.

ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve
ask_manager_for.use_when: The current cut or frontier context may expose a call route.

ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations
ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.

ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier
ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible cut may depend on LHS/RHS statement alignment or missing live facts.

ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps
ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, seq, while, rnd, or rewrite.
The visible cut/frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch tactics.

ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps
ask_manager_for.use_when: Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.

ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps
ask_manager_for.use_when: A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.

ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery
ask_manager_for.use_when: A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.
